failure_modes.text: Practice can become fake progress when intense drills are disconnected from the real failure modes and feedback conditions of live performance.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when practice is intense but not reality-linked.
failure_modes.mitigation: Connect drills to the actual failure modes and feedback conditions of live performance; practice that never encounters real-world friction is fake progress.

failure_modes.text: A flawed mental model can become more dangerous when repetition entrenches the error instead of exposing it to corrective feedback.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair repetition with corrective feedback: a flawed mental model becomes more dangerous when entrenched through practice without correction.

failure_modes.text: A well-practiced tool can get overapplied to novel problems simply because it is the one the practitioner has most rehearsed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether the most-rehearsed tool is being applied outside its domain; familiarity from practice can create Maslow's hammer.

premortem_questions.text: What assumptions are implicit in the framework or drill I am currently using, and are those assumptions met here?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What assumptions are implicit in the frameworks I am currently using, and are those assumptions met in this new context?

premortem_questions.text: What key perspective from outside my practiced domain am I neglecting that might expose a blind spot?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What key perspective (from a discipline outside my domain of expertise) am I neglecting that might uncover a blind spot in my thinking?

premortem_questions.text: Can I explain this concept simply enough that a novice would understand it, and if not, where are my knowledge gaps?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Can I explain this concept simply enough that a five-year-old would understand it? If not, where are my knowledge gaps?

heuristics.text: Define improvement against a tighter benchmark, coach review, or replayable standard so practice stays measurable over time.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when progress must be measurable over time.

heuristics.text: Break capability into sub-skills so drills target the actual weak component instead of vague overall effort.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when the bottleneck is sub-skill quality.

heuristics.text: Pull in outside views and alternative frames so repeated practice sharpens reality contact instead of entrenching one favored model.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on measurable practice, sub-skill targeting, and anti-entrenchment discipline rather than on generic effort rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor training-program sprawl because the raw source is already strong on measurable reps, sub-skill targeting, and corrective feedback.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several outside-view and anti-entrenchment passages so it stays reusable as a compact intervention chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems-thinking, feedback-loops, and reflective learning for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists and tension sections are strongest on confirmation-bias, status-quo-bias, and commitment-bias.
structured_tensions: The source contains an explicit structured tension block that should survive into Wave 3 with bounded normalization.
